113	/** Generate a small noise texture, return data URL */
114	let noiseDataURL = '';
115	function generateNoiseTexture() {
116	  const size = 512;
117	  const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
118	  canvas.width = size;
119	  canvas.height = size;
120	  const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
121	  const img = ctx.createImageData(size, size);
122	  for (let i = 0; i < img.data.length; i += 4) {
123	    const v = Math.floor(Math.random() * 255);
124	    img.data[i] = v;
125	    img.data[i + 1] = v;
126	    img.data[i + 2] = v;
127	    img.data[i + 3] = 255;
128	  }
129	  ctx.putImageData(img, 0, 0);
130	  noiseDataURL = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
131	}
132

304	/** Build pagination HTML and wire up listeners */
305	function createPaginationNav(id) {
306	  const totalSlides = currentContent ? currentContent.slides.length : 0;
307	  const totalPages = Math.ceil(totalSlides / SLIDES_PER_PAGE);
308	
309	  const nav = document.createElement('div');
310	  nav.id = id;
311	  nav.className = 'pagination';
312	
313	  const start = currentPage * SLIDES_PER_PAGE + 1;
314	  const end = Math.min(start + SLIDES_PER_PAGE - 1, totalSlides);
315	
316	  nav.innerHTML = `
317	    <button class="pg-btn pg-prev" ${currentPage === 0 ? 'disabled' : ''}>&larr; Prev</button>
318	    <span class="pg-info">Slides ${start}–${end} of ${totalSlides}</span>
319	    <button class="pg-btn pg-next" ${currentPage >= totalPages - 1 ? 'disabled' : ''}>Next &rarr;</button>
320	  `;
321	
322	  nav.querySelector('.pg-prev').addEventListener('click', () => {
323	    if (currentPage > 0) goToPage(currentPage - 1, 'back');
324	  });
325	  nav.querySelector('.pg-next').addEventListener('click', () => {
326	    if (currentPage < totalPages - 1) goToPage(currentPage + 1, 'forward');
327	  });
328	
329	  return nav;
330	}

332	/** Render pagination controls above and below the deck */
333	function renderPagination() {
334	  const totalSlides = currentContent ? currentContent.slides.length : 0;
335	  const totalPages = Math.ceil(totalSlides / SLIDES_PER_PAGE);
336	
337	  // Remove existing pagination elements

339	  document.getElementById('pagination-bottom')?.remove();

350	/** Generate a pinstripe tile as a data URL for html2canvas export */
351	function generatePinstripeTexture(direction, color) {
352	  const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
353	  const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
354	
355	  if (direction === 'diagonal') {
356	    const size = 8;
357	    canvas.width = size;
358	    canvas.height = size;
359	    ctx.strokeStyle = color;
360	    ctx.lineWidth = 1;
361	    ctx.beginPath();
362	    ctx.moveTo(size, 0);
363	    ctx.lineTo(0, size);
364	    ctx.stroke();
365	    // Wrap the line so the tile repeats seamlessly
366	    ctx.beginPath();
367	    ctx.moveTo(size + size, 0);
368	    ctx.lineTo(0, size + size);
369	    ctx.stroke();
370	  } else {
371	    canvas.width = direction === 'vertical' ? 9 : 1;
372	    canvas.height = direction === 'vertical' ? 1 : 9;
373	    ctx.fillStyle = color;
374	    if (direction === 'vertical') {
375	      ctx.fillRect(8, 0, 1, 1);
376	    } else {
377	      ctx.fillRect(0, 8, 1, 1);
378	    }
379	  }
380	
381	  return canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
382	}
383	
384	/** Export a single slide as a PNG download */
385	async function exportSlide(page, type) {
386	  const slideEl = document.getElementById(`slide-${page}`);
387	  if (!slideEl) return;
388	
389	  const btn = slideEl.closest('.slide-wrapper').querySelector('.slide-export-btn');
390	  btn.textContent = 'Rendering…';
391	  btn.disabled = true;
392	
393	  try {
394	    const { default: html2canvas } = await import('html2canvas');
395	    const canvas = await html2canvas(slideEl, {
396	      scale: 2,
397	      useCORS: true,
398	      logging: false,
399	      backgroundColor: null,
400	    });
401	
402	    const link = document.createElement('a');
403	    link.download = `slide-${String(page).padStart(2, '0')}-${type}.png`;
404	    link.href = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
405	    link.click();
406	  } catch (e) {
407	    console.error('Export failed:', e);
408	  }
409	
410	  btn.textContent = 'Export PNG';
411	  btn.disabled = false;
412	}

414	/** Export all slides as PNGs saved to the export/ folder on disk */
415	async function exportAllSlides() {
416	  const btn = document.getElementById('btn-export-all');
417	  if (!btn) return;
418	  btn.disabled = true;
419	
420	  const totalSlides = currentContent.slides.length;
421	  const savedPage = currentPage;
422	  const totalPages = Math.ceil(totalSlides / SLIDES_PER_PAGE);
423	  const { default: html2canvas } = await import('html2canvas');
424	  const pendingSaves = [];
425	
426	  /** Capture all visible slides and fire save requests */
427	  async function capturePage(pageSlides, startIdx, subdir) {
428	    const capturePromises = pageSlides.map((slide, j) => {
429	      const i = startIdx + j;
430	      const page = i + 1;
431	      const slideEl = document.getElementById(`slide-${page}`);
432	      if (!slideEl) return Promise.resolve();
433	
434	      const slideWidth = slideEl.offsetWidth;
435	      const exportScale = Math.ceil(3840 / slideWidth);
436	      return html2canvas(slideEl, {
437	        scale: exportScale,
438	        useCORS: true,
439	        logging: false,
440	        backgroundColor: null,
441	      }).then(canvas => ({
442	        filename: `slide-${String(page).padStart(2, '0')}-${slide.type}.png`,
443	        dataUrl: canvas.toDataURL('image/png'),
444	      })).catch(e => {
445	        console.error(`Capture slide ${page} failed:`, e);
446	        return null;
447	      });
448	    });
449	
450	    const captured = (await Promise.all(capturePromises)).filter(Boolean);
451	
452	    const savePromises = captured.map(({ filename, dataUrl }) =>
453	      fetch('/api/save-slide', {
454	        method: 'POST',
455	        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
456	        body: JSON.stringify({ filename, dataUrl, subdir }),
457	      }).catch(e => console.error(`Save ${filename} failed:`, e))
458	    );
459	
460	    pendingSaves.push(...savePromises);
461	  }
462	
463	  for (let pg = 0; pg < totalPages; pg++) {
464	    setPage(pg);
465	    renderDeck();
466	    fixStatBlockContrast();
467	    fitStatSizes();
468	
469	    // Wait for all WebGL shapes to finish rendering
470	    await deckPostInitsReady;
471	
472	    const startIdx = pg * SLIDES_PER_PAGE;
473	    const pageSlides = currentContent.slides.slice(startIdx, startIdx + SLIDES_PER_PAGE);
474	
475	    // ── 16:9 capture ──
476	    btn.textContent = `16:9 page ${pg + 1}/${totalPages}…`;
477	    await capturePage(pageSlides, startIdx, '16x9');
478	
479	    // ── 4:3 capture — full re-render at 4:3 so droplets get fresh content textures ──
480	    btn.textContent = `4:3 page ${pg + 1}/${totalPages}…`;
481	    document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--export-aspect', '4 / 3');
482	    renderDeck();
483	    fixStatBlockContrast();
484	    fitStatSizes();
485	    await deckPostInitsReady;
486	    await capturePage(pageSlides, startIdx, '4x3');
487	    document.documentElement.style.removeProperty('--export-aspect');
488	  }
489	
490	  // Wait for all saves to finish before building the deck
491	  btn.textContent = `Optimizing PNGs…`;
492	  await Promise.all(pendingSaves);
493	
494	  // Build PPTX and PDF from the exported PNGs
495	  btn.textContent = 'Building PPTX + PDF…';
496	  try {
497	    const buildRes = await fetch('/api/build-deck', { method: 'POST' });
498	    const buildData = await buildRes.json();
499	    if (buildData.ok) {
500	      btn.textContent = `Done — ${buildData.slides} slides`;
501	    } else {
502	      btn.textContent = 'PNG done, deck failed';
503	      console.error('Build deck failed:', buildData.error);
504	    }
505	  } catch (e) {
506	    console.error('Build deck failed:', e);
507	    btn.textContent = 'PNG done, deck failed';
508	  }
509	
510	  // Restore original page
511	  setPage(savedPage);
512	  renderDeck();
513	  setTimeout(() => { btn.textContent = 'Export All'; btn.disabled = false; }, 3000);
514	}

724	/** Re-render a single slide in place (without tearing down the whole deck) */
725	function renderSingleSlide(slideIndex) {
726	  const config = currentConfig;
727	  const slide = currentContent.slides[slideIndex];
728	  const page = slideIndex + 1;
729	
730	  const slideEl = document.getElementById(`slide-${page}`);
731	  if (!slideEl) return;
732	
733	  const layoutName = config.layouts[slide.type] || Object.values(config.layouts)[0];
734	  const layoutFn = layoutRegistry[layoutName];
735	  if (!layoutFn) return;
736	
737	  slideEl.innerHTML = layoutFn(slide, page);
738	
739	  const inner = slideEl.querySelector('.slide-inner');
740	  if (!inner) return;
741	
742	  // Re-inject textures (shapes are NOT re-injected to avoid WebGL churn)
743	  if (config.pinstripes !== 'none') {
744	    inner.insertAdjacentHTML('afterbegin', '<div class="shape-overlay texture-pinstripes"></div>');
745	  }
746	  inner.insertAdjacentHTML(
747	    'afterbegin',
748	    `<div class="shape-overlay texture-noise" style="background-image:url(${noiseDataURL});opacity:${config.noise / 100}"></div>`,
749	  );
750	
751	  // Fix stat block contrast if this is a data slide
752	  slideEl.querySelectorAll('.stat-block').forEach(block => {
753	    const blockL = luminance(getComputedStyle(block).backgroundColor);
754	    block.style.color = blockL > 0.18 ? '#1B2A3D' : '#FFFFFF';
755	    const inner2 = block.closest('.slide-inner');
756	    if (inner2) {
757	      const slideL = luminance(getComputedStyle(inner2).backgroundColor);
758	      block.style.border = Math.abs(blockL - slideL) < 0.05 ? '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.15)' : '';
759	    }
760	  });
761	
762	  // Fit stat value sizes for this slide — all match the longest text
763	  const statValues = slideEl.querySelectorAll('.stat-value');
764	  if (statValues.length > 0) {
765	    statValues.forEach(v => { v.style.fontSize = ''; v.style.whiteSpace = 'nowrap'; });
766	    let minFitted = Infinity;
767	    statValues.forEach(v => {
768	      const block = v.closest('.stat-block');
769	      const padL = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(block).paddingLeft);
770	      const padR = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(block).paddingRight);
771	      const available = block.clientWidth - padL - padR;
772	      const currentSize = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(v).fontSize);
773	      const textWidth = v.scrollWidth;
774	      if (textWidth > 0) minFitted = Math.min(minFitted, currentSize * (available / textWidth));
775	    });
776	    if (minFitted !== Infinity) {
777	      const firstBlock = statValues[0].closest('.stat-block');
778	      const blockH = firstBlock.clientHeight - parseFloat(getComputedStyle(firstBlock).paddingTop) - parseFloat(getComputedStyle(firstBlock).paddingBottom);
779	      const finalSize = Math.min(minFitted, blockH * 0.55);
780	      statValues.forEach(v => v.style.fontSize = `${finalSize}px`);
781	    }
782	  }
783	}
784	
785	/** Relative luminance from an rgb() or rgba() string */
786	function luminance(cssColor) {
787	  const match = cssColor.match(/(\d+),\s*(\d+),\s*(\d+)/);
788	  if (!match) return 0;
789	  const [r, g, b] = [match[1], match[2], match[3]].map(c => {
790	    const s = Number(c) / 255;
791	    return s <= 0.03928 ? s / 12.92 : Math.pow((s + 0.055) / 1.055, 2.4);
792	  });
793	  return 0.2126 * r + 0.7152 * g + 0.0722 * b;
794	}
795	
796	/** Scale stat values so the longest text fills its block width, and all others match that size */
797	function fitStatSizes() {
798	  document.querySelectorAll('.data-big-numbers').forEach(slide => {
799	    const values = slide.querySelectorAll('.stat-value');
800	    if (values.length === 0) return;
801	
802	    // Reset and prevent wrapping for measurement
803	    values.forEach(v => { v.style.fontSize = ''; v.style.whiteSpace = 'nowrap'; });
804	
805	    // Find the size that makes the longest text exactly fill its block
806	    let minFitted = Infinity;
807	    values.forEach(v => {
808	      const block = v.closest('.stat-block');
809	      const padL = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(block).paddingLeft);
810	      const padR = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(block).paddingRight);
811	      const available = block.clientWidth - padL - padR;
812	      const currentSize = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(v).fontSize);
813	      const textWidth = v.scrollWidth;
814	      if (textWidth > 0) {
815	        minFitted = Math.min(minFitted, currentSize * (available / textWidth));
816	      }
817	    });
818	
819	    if (minFitted !== Infinity) {
820	      // Cap at 55% of block height so label text still fits
821	      const firstBlock = values[0].closest('.stat-block');
822	      const blockH = firstBlock.clientHeight - parseFloat(getComputedStyle(firstBlock).paddingTop) - parseFloat(getComputedStyle(firstBlock).paddingBottom);
823	      const finalSize = Math.min(minFitted, blockH * 0.55);
824	      values.forEach(v => v.style.fontSize = `${finalSize}px`);
825	    }
826	  });
827	}

835	    // Add border when block blends into slide background

1	import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
2	import fs from 'fs';
3	import path from 'path';
4	import sharp from 'sharp';
5	
6	/** Helper: backup a file with a timestamp, then write new data */
7	function backupAndWrite(filePath, data, ts) {
8	  const ext = path.extname(filePath);
9	  const base = path.basename(filePath, ext);
10	  const dir = path.dirname(filePath);
11	  const backupPath = path.join(dir, `${base}_${ts}${ext}`);
12	  if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
13	    fs.copyFileSync(filePath, backupPath);
14	  }
15	  fs.writeFileSync(filePath, JSON.stringify(data, null, 2) + '\n');
16	  return path.basename(backupPath);
17	}
18	
19	/** Vite plugin: save content.json + config.json via POST /api/save */
20	function savePlugin() {
21	  return {
22	    name: 'deck-save',
23	    configureServer(server) {
24	      // Keep the old endpoint working
25	      server.middlewares.use('/api/save-content', async (req, res) => {
26	        if (req.method !== 'POST') { res.statusCode = 405; res.end('Method not allowed'); return; }
27	        let body = ''; for await (const chunk of req) body += chunk;
28	        try {
29	          const json = JSON.parse(body);
30	          const ts = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-').slice(0, 19);
31	          const backup = backupAndWrite(path.resolve('content.json'), json, ts);
32	          res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json');
33	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, backup }));
34	        } catch (e) { res.statusCode = 400; res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: e.message })); }
35	      });
36	
37	      // Save a PNG slide image to disk
38	      server.middlewares.use('/api/save-slide', async (req, res) => {
39	        if (req.method !== 'POST') { res.statusCode = 405; res.end('Method not allowed'); return; }
40	        const chunks = [];
41	        for await (const chunk of req) chunks.push(chunk);
42	        const body = Buffer.concat(chunks);
43	        try {
44	          // Expect JSON with { filename, dataUrl }
45	          const { filename, dataUrl, subdir } = JSON.parse(body.toString());
46	          const exportDir = subdir
47	            ? path.resolve('export', subdir)
48	            : path.resolve('export');
49	          if (!fs.existsSync(exportDir)) fs.mkdirSync(exportDir, { recursive: true });
50	          const base64 = dataUrl.replace(/^data:image\/png;base64,/, '');
51	          const rawBuf = Buffer.from(base64, 'base64');
52	          const filePath = path.join(exportDir, filename);
53	
54	          // Lossless PNG optimization via sharp — better compression, same pixels
55	          const optimized = await sharp(rawBuf)
56	            .png({ compressionLevel: 9, adaptiveFiltering: true })
57	            .toBuffer();
58	
59	          const saved = Math.round((1 - optimized.length / rawBuf.length) * 100);
60	          fs.writeFileSync(filePath, optimized);
61	          res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json');
62	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, path: `export/${filename}`, saved: `${saved}%` }));
63	        } catch (e) { res.statusCode = 400; res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: e.message })); }
64	      });
65	
66	      // Build PPTX and PDF from exported PNGs
67	      server.middlewares.use('/api/build-deck', async (req, res) => {
68	        if (req.method !== 'POST') { res.statusCode = 405; res.end('Method not allowed'); return; }
69	        try {
70	          const PptxGenJS = (await import('pptxgenjs')).default;
71	          const PDFDocument = (await import('pdfkit')).default;
72	
73	          async function buildDeck(dir, widthIn, heightIn, label) {
74	            const pngs = fs.readdirSync(dir)
75	              .filter(f => f.endsWith('.png') && f.startsWith('slide-'))
76	              .sort();
77	            if (pngs.length === 0) return null;
78	
79	            // ── PPTX ──
80	            const pptx = new PptxGenJS();
81	            pptx.defineLayout({ name: 'CUSTOM', width: widthIn, height: heightIn });
82	            pptx.layout = 'CUSTOM';
83	
84	            for (const png of pngs) {
85	              const slide = pptx.addSlide();
86	              const b64 = fs.readFileSync(path.join(dir, png)).toString('base64');
87	              slide.addImage({
88	                data: `image/png;base64,${b64}`,
89	                x: 0, y: 0, w: '100%', h: '100%',
90	              });
91	            }
92	            await pptx.writeFile({ fileName: path.join(dir, 'presentation.pptx') });
93	
94	            // ── PDF ──
95	            const pageW = widthIn * 72;
96	            const pageH = heightIn * 72;
97	            await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
98	              const doc = new PDFDocument({ size: [pageW, pageH], autoFirstPage: false, margin: 0 });
99	              const stream = fs.createWriteStream(path.join(dir, 'presentation.pdf'));
100	              doc.pipe(stream);
101	              for (const png of pngs) {
102	                doc.addPage({ size: [pageW, pageH], margin: 0 });
103	                doc.image(path.join(dir, png), 0, 0, { width: pageW, height: pageH });
104	              }
105	              doc.end();
106	              stream.on('finish', resolve);
107	              stream.on('error', reject);
108	            });
109	
110	            return pngs.length;
111	          }
112	
113	          const exportDir = path.resolve('export');
114	          const dir16x9 = path.join(exportDir, '16x9');
115	          const dir4x3 = path.join(exportDir, '4x3');
116	          const results = {};
117	
118	          // 16:9 — 13.333 × 7.5 inches
119	          if (fs.existsSync(dir16x9)) {
120	            const count16 = await buildDeck(dir16x9, 13.333, 7.5, '16:9');
121	            if (count16) results['16:9'] = count16;
122	          }
123	
124	          // 4:3 — 10 × 7.5 inches
125	          if (fs.existsSync(dir4x3)) {
126	            const count43 = await buildDeck(dir4x3, 10, 7.5, '4:3');
127	            if (count43) results['4:3'] = count43;
128	          }
129	
130	          res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json');
131	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, results }));
132	        } catch (e) {
133	          res.statusCode = 500;
134	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: e.message }));
135	        }
136	      });
137	
138	      // New combined endpoint
139	      server.middlewares.use('/api/save', async (req, res) => {
140	        if (req.method !== 'POST') { res.statusCode = 405; res.end('Method not allowed'); return; }
141	        let body = ''; for await (const chunk of req) body += chunk;
142	        try {
143	          const { content, config } = JSON.parse(body);
144	          const ts = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-').slice(0, 19);
145	          const backups = [];
146	          if (content) backups.push(backupAndWrite(path.resolve('content.json'), content, ts));
147	          if (config)  backups.push(backupAndWrite(path.resolve('config.json'), config, ts));
148	          res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json');
149	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, backups }));
150	        } catch (e) { res.statusCode = 400; res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: e.message })); }
151	      });
152	    },
153	  };
154	}

162	});

I have enough context. Here's the plan — no code changes yet.

## Plan: Per-slide voiceover recording

### What gets added
A small audio control row inside each slide's label bar (next to **Edit / Duplicate / Export PNG**). Per slide it shows:

- **Record** button → toggles to **Stop** while recording (red dot indicator)
- **Play** button (▶) → preview the saved take
- **Duration** label → e.g. `0:42` (or `—` if none yet)
- **Delete** button (✕) → clear the take
- **Re-record** is just hitting Record again (with a confirm if a take exists)

Same controls also appear in **Play mode** so you can rehearse with audio.

### Data model — `content.json`
Add an optional `voiceover` field per slide:
```json
"voiceover": { "file": "voiceovers/slide-03.webm", "duration": 42.7, "mime": "audio/webm", "updated": "2026-04-13T..." }
```
Stored on disk under `deck-generator/voiceovers/slide-NN.webm` (NN = 1-based page index, padded). Duration is cached so the UI can render instantly without decoding the file.

### Recording (browser side, `src/main.js`)
- Use the **MediaRecorder API** with `getUserMedia({ audio: true })` — no new dependencies.
- Prefer `audio/webm;codecs=opus` (universal in Chrome/Edge/Firefox); fall back to `audio/mp4` on Safari. Store the chosen MIME in the slide's `voiceover.mime` so playback knows what to load.
- One shared `mediaStream` reused across slides; release tracks when no slide is recording.
- On Stop: assemble the Blob, decode with an `AudioContext` to get exact duration, then POST to a new `/api/save-voiceover` endpoint.
- Only one slide can be recording at a time — starting a new one stops the previous.
- Microphone permission is requested lazily (first Record click), with a clear error if denied.

### Server side (`vite.config.js`)
Two new endpoints inside `savePlugin`:

1. **`POST /api/save-voiceover`** — body `{ slideIndex, dataUrl, mime, duration }`.
   - Decode base64 to a Buffer, write to `voiceovers/slide-NN.<ext>` (ext from MIME).
   - Update `currentContent.slides[slideIndex].voiceover` and write `content.json` (reuse `backupAndWrite` so a timestamped backup is made).
   - Return `{ ok, file, duration }`.
2. **`DELETE /api/voiceover/:slideIndex`** — remove the file and clear the field on the slide, also via `backupAndWrite`.

Static serving: Vite already serves project files, so `voiceovers/slide-03.webm` will be reachable as `/voiceovers/slide-03.webm` for `<audio>` playback.

Add `voiceovers/` to `.gitignore` (or commit them — your call; flagging because audio files bloat git).

### UI wiring (`src/main.js`)
- New helper `buildVoiceoverControls(slide, slideIndex)` returning a DOM fragment with the four buttons + duration span.
- Hooked into `renderDeck()` after the existing `exportBtn` append (around `main.js:940`) and into `renderPlaySlide()` (`main.js:1108`) for the play overlay.
- A small `formatDuration(seconds)` util → `m:ss`.
- Recording state held in a module-level object `{ slideIndex, recorder, chunks, startedAt }`; UI updates via `requestAnimationFrame` to show a live timer while recording.

### Styling (`src/style.css`)
- Reuse `.slide-export-btn` for buttons; add `.voiceover-row`, `.voiceover-duration`, and a `.voiceover-recording` modifier (red border + pulsing dot) — ~30 lines, slotted in next to the existing `.slide-export-btn` rules at line 355.

### Edge cases I'll handle
- Slide reordering / duplication: the file path is keyed by slide index, so **duplicate** must currently *not* copy the audio (clone would alias). I'll either copy the file with a new index, or null out `voiceover` on the clone — I'll go with **null on clone** unless you prefer the copy.
- HTTPS / `localhost`: `getUserMedia` works on `localhost` so dev is fine; flag if you ever serve over plain HTTP from another host.
- Existing `Export All` and PPTX/PDF builders ignore audio entirely — no changes needed there.

### Out of scope (flagging in case you want them)
- Embedding audio into PPTX/PDF exports.
- Waveform visualisation.
- Trim / re-record-from-X.

---

**Open questions before I build it:**
1. **Duplicate behaviour** — clone audio, or leave the copy with no take? (I'd default to no take.)
2. **`voiceovers/` in git** — ignore or commit?
3. **Total duration** — want a "total deck runtime" readout in the top controls bar too?

Perfect. Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the findings:

---

## Verification Report

### 1. **Slide Identity / Stable ID**
Slides **do not have any stable identifier**. They are keyed only by array index (`i` in `renderDeck`). The slide objects in `content.json` have no `id`, `uuid`, or `slug` fields—only `type`, content properties, and a `notes` object.

**Critical: Duplicate operation breaks index keying.** Line 918 in `main.js` shows the duplicate handler uses `splice(i + 1, 0, clone)`, which shifts all subsequent indices. The code then mirrors this for `slideShapes` overrides (lines 920–927), showing that the app assumes **per-slide metadata is keyed by index and must be shifted on insertion/deletion.**

**Conclusion:** Keying audio files by index will break on reorder/duplicate unless you either (a) add stable UUIDs to each slide, or (b) shift the audio file keys the same way `slideShapes` is shifted in the duplicate handler (lines 920–927).

---

### 2. **Static File Serving**
`vite.config.js` (lines 156–162) has:
- `root: '.'` (project root)
- No `publicDir` or `server.fs` restrictions specified

**Vite default:** serves the project root as static files. A directory `deck-generator/voiceovers/` would indeed be reachable as `/voiceovers/...` without configuration.

**No `.gitignore` exists** in the deck-generator directory (checked; file not found). You'll need to add one if you want to exclude voiceovers from version control.

---

### 3. **Play Mode UI Structure**
`enterPlayMode()` (lines 1032–1049) creates the overlay and renders it. `renderPlaySlide()` (lines 1061–1133) populates:
- `#play-label` (line 1109–1110): slide metadata at top-left
- `#play-slide` (line 1104–1106): the slide content (inside `.play-slide-container`)
- `#play-counter` (line 1112–1113): pagination info at bottom-right

**Keyboard:** Only Arrow Left/Right and Escape are bound (lines 1144–1152). **Spacebar is unbound** in play mode; no conflict there.

**CSS positions** (lines 1364–1382):
- `.play-label`: `position: fixed; top: 1.2rem; left: 1.5rem; pointer-events: none`
- `.play-counter`: `position: fixed; bottom: 1.2rem; right: 1.5rem; pointer-events: none`

**Suggested placement for per-slide audio controls:** Bottom-left corner (unused quadrant), or as an overlay button inside the slide container (above the counter). The current layout has top-left and bottom-right reserved.

---

### 4. **Slide Label Row Append Order**
Lines 936–940 in `main.js`:
1. `labelText` (slide number + type + layout name)
2. `shapeSelect` (dropdown for shape override)
3. `editBtn` (Edit/Close toggle)
4. `dupeBtn` (Duplicate)
5. `exportBtn` (Export PNG)

The `shapeSelect` is positioned with `margin-left: auto` (line 339 in style.css), pushing buttons to the right. A new voiceover button should slot **between `dupeBtn` and `exportBtn`** or **before `shapeSelect`** depending on intent.

---

### 5. **CSS Classes for Label Row & Play Overlay**

| Class | File:Line | Description |
|-------|-----------|-------------|
| `.slide-label` | `src/style.css:254` | `display: flex; justify-content: space-between` container for slide controls |
| `.slide-export-btn` | `src/style.css:355` | Generic button style (border, transparent bg, hover highlight) |
| `.slide-export-btn:hover` | `src/style.css:368` | Color/border highlight on hover |
| `.slide-export-btn:disabled` | `src/style.css:373` | Opacity 0.5, cursor: default |
| `#play-overlay` | `src/style.css:1334` | `position: fixed; inset: 0; z-index: 9999; background: #000` (fullscreen) |
| `.play-label` | `src/style.css:1364` | `position: fixed; top/left` metadata text, `pointer-events: none` |
| `.play-counter` | `src/style.css:1374` | `position: fixed; bottom/right` pagination, `pointer-events: none` |

Add voiceover CSS after line 1382 (end of `.play-counter` rule).

---

### 6. **Server-Side Write Pattern**
Your read is **correct.** The `/api/save` endpoint (lines 139–151) accepts a single POST with both `content` and `config`, and overwrites them atomically via `backupAndWrite()`.

For audio + metadata:
- **Add a new `/api/save-voiceover` endpoint** that takes binary audio + filename, writes to `voiceovers/` directory
- **Do NOT reuse `backupAndWrite()`** for the audio endpoint—it's JSON-only (line 15)
- **Keep `/api/save` for content.json updates** (e.g., updating `slide.audioUrl`)
- Client flow: (1) record → (2) POST to `/api/save-voiceover` → (3) update `slide.audioUrl` in `currentContent` → (4) POST to `/api/save` with updated content.json

The current `backupAndWrite()` creates timestamped backups of JSON. For audio files, you'd likely skip backups (disk space) or use a different strategy (version in filename or separate archive).

---

### 7. **Existing Notes Editor Pattern & Other Details**

**Notes pattern (lines 548–675):**
- Notes are stored on each slide as `slide.notes = { transition: string, points: array }`
- Built via `ensureSlideNotes()` helper (line 549) which initializes missing structure
- Panel built in `buildEditPanel()` with header "Speaker Notes", help text, and textarea fields for `transition` and individual `points`

**UX pattern to mirror:** The notes section sits at the bottom of the edit panel (grid-column: 1/-1), with a header and optional help text. A voiceover recorder could follow the same pattern—header + recorder UI + help text.

**Directory conventions:**
- `export/` — PNG exports (line 47, organized by aspect ratio: `export/16x9/`, `export/4x3/`)
- `output/` — Exists but not actively used in the codebase (likely for build artifacts)
- Suggested: `voiceovers/` at root level, flat structure keyed by slide index: `voiceovers/slide-001.webm`, etc., or by stable slide ID if you add UUIDs

**No existing audio infrastructure.** MediaRecorder and audio playback are not currently in the codebase.

---

## Summary for Implementation

**Critical constraint:** Slides are keyed by index only. Adding audio requires either (a) shifting indices on duplicate (mirror the `slideShapes` pattern), or (b) add stable UUIDs to all slides in `content.json`.

**Endpoints to add:** `/api/save-voiceover` (binary audio write) + reuse `/api/save` for content.json updates.

**Play mode UI space:** Bottom-left or inside slide container; top-left and bottom-right are reserved for metadata.

**Edit mode UI:** Slot voiceover control in label row (between duplicate & export buttons) OR below the notes panel in the edit pane following the notes UX pattern.

1	import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
2	import fs from 'fs';
3	import path from 'path';
4	import sharp from 'sharp';
5	
6	/** Helper: backup a file with a timestamp, then write new data */
7	function backupAndWrite(filePath, data, ts) {
8	  const ext = path.extname(filePath);
9	  const base = path.basename(filePath, ext);
10	  const dir = path.dirname(filePath);
11	  const backupPath = path.join(dir, `${base}_${ts}${ext}`);
12	  if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
13	    fs.copyFileSync(filePath, backupPath);
14	  }
15	  fs.writeFileSync(filePath, JSON.stringify(data, null, 2) + '\n');
16	  return path.basename(backupPath);
17	}
18	
19	/** Vite plugin: save content.json + config.json via POST /api/save */
20	function savePlugin() {
21	  return {
22	    name: 'deck-save',
23	    configureServer(server) {
24	      // Keep the old endpoint working
25	      server.middlewares.use('/api/save-content', async (req, res) => {
26	        if (req.method !== 'POST') { res.statusCode = 405; res.end('Method not allowed'); return; }
27	        let body = ''; for await (const chunk of req) body += chunk;
28	        try {
29	          const json = JSON.parse(body);
30	          const ts = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-').slice(0, 19);
31	          const backup = backupAndWrite(path.resolve('content.json'), json, ts);
32	          res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json');
33	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, backup }));
34	        } catch (e) { res.statusCode = 400; res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: e.message })); }
35	      });
36	
37	      // Save a PNG slide image to disk
38	      server.middlewares.use('/api/save-slide', async (req, res) => {
39	        if (req.method !== 'POST') { res.statusCode = 405; res.end('Method not allowed'); return; }
40	        const chunks = [];
41	        for await (const chunk of req) chunks.push(chunk);
42	        const body = Buffer.concat(chunks);
43	        try {
44	          // Expect JSON with { filename, dataUrl }
45	          const { filename, dataUrl, subdir } = JSON.parse(body.toString());
46	          const exportDir = subdir
47	            ? path.resolve('export', subdir)
48	            : path.resolve('export');
49	          if (!fs.existsSync(exportDir)) fs.mkdirSync(exportDir, { recursive: true });
50	          const base64 = dataUrl.replace(/^data:image\/png;base64,/, '');
51	          const rawBuf = Buffer.from(base64, 'base64');
52	          const filePath = path.join(exportDir, filename);
53	
54	          // Lossless PNG optimization via sharp — better compression, same pixels
55	          const optimized = await sharp(rawBuf)
56	            .png({ compressionLevel: 9, adaptiveFiltering: true })
57	            .toBuffer();
58	
59	          const saved = Math.round((1 - optimized.length / rawBuf.length) * 100);
60	          fs.writeFileSync(filePath, optimized);
61	          res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json');
62	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, path: `export/${filename}`, saved: `${saved}%` }));
63	        } catch (e) { res.statusCode = 400; res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: e.message })); }
64	      });
65	
66	      // Build PPTX and PDF from exported PNGs
67	      server.middlewares.use('/api/build-deck', async (req, res) => {
68	        if (req.method !== 'POST') { res.statusCode = 405; res.end('Method not allowed'); return; }
69	        try {
70	          const PptxGenJS = (await import('pptxgenjs')).default;
71	          const PDFDocument = (await import('pdfkit')).default;
72	
73	          async function buildDeck(dir, widthIn, heightIn, label) {
74	            const pngs = fs.readdirSync(dir)
75	              .filter(f => f.endsWith('.png') && f.startsWith('slide-'))
76	              .sort();
77	            if (pngs.length === 0) return null;
78	
79	            // ── PPTX ──
80	            const pptx = new PptxGenJS();
81	            pptx.defineLayout({ name: 'CUSTOM', width: widthIn, height: heightIn });
82	            pptx.layout = 'CUSTOM';
83	
84	            for (const png of pngs) {
85	              const slide = pptx.addSlide();
86	              const b64 = fs.readFileSync(path.join(dir, png)).toString('base64');
87	              slide.addImage({
88	                data: `image/png;base64,${b64}`,
89	                x: 0, y: 0, w: '100%', h: '100%',
90	              });
91	            }
92	            await pptx.writeFile({ fileName: path.join(dir, 'presentation.pptx') });
93	
94	            // ── PDF ──
95	            const pageW = widthIn * 72;
96	            const pageH = heightIn * 72;
97	            await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
98	              const doc = new PDFDocument({ size: [pageW, pageH], autoFirstPage: false, margin: 0 });
99	              const stream = fs.createWriteStream(path.join(dir, 'presentation.pdf'));
100	              doc.pipe(stream);
101	              for (const png of pngs) {
102	                doc.addPage({ size: [pageW, pageH], margin: 0 });
103	                doc.image(path.join(dir, png), 0, 0, { width: pageW, height: pageH });
104	              }
105	              doc.end();
106	              stream.on('finish', resolve);
107	              stream.on('error', reject);
108	            });
109	
110	            return pngs.length;
111	          }
112	
113	          const exportDir = path.resolve('export');
114	          const dir16x9 = path.join(exportDir, '16x9');
115	          const dir4x3 = path.join(exportDir, '4x3');
116	          const results = {};
117	
118	          // 16:9 — 13.333 × 7.5 inches
119	          if (fs.existsSync(dir16x9)) {
120	            const count16 = await buildDeck(dir16x9, 13.333, 7.5, '16:9');
121	            if (count16) results['16:9'] = count16;
122	          }
123	
124	          // 4:3 — 10 × 7.5 inches
125	          if (fs.existsSync(dir4x3)) {
126	            const count43 = await buildDeck(dir4x3, 10, 7.5, '4:3');
127	            if (count43) results['4:3'] = count43;
128	          }
129	
130	          res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json');
131	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, results }));
132	        } catch (e) {
133	          res.statusCode = 500;
134	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: e.message }));
135	        }
136	      });
137	
138	      // New combined endpoint
139	      server.middlewares.use('/api/save', async (req, res) => {
140	        if (req.method !== 'POST') { res.statusCode = 405; res.end('Method not allowed'); return; }
141	        let body = ''; for await (const chunk of req) body += chunk;
142	        try {
143	          const { content, config } = JSON.parse(body);
144	          const ts = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-').slice(0, 19);
145	          const backups = [];
146	          if (content) backups.push(backupAndWrite(path.resolve('content.json'), content, ts));
147	          if (config)  backups.push(backupAndWrite(path.resolve('config.json'), config, ts));
148	          res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json');
149	          res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, backups }));
150	        } catch (e) { res.statusCode = 400; res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: e.message })); }
151	      });
152	    },
153	  };
154	}
155	
156	export default defineConfig({
157	  root: '.',
158	  server: {
159	    open: true,
160	  },
161	  plugins: [savePlugin()],
162	});
163

---

## Verification Report

### 1. **Slide Identity / Stable ID**
Slides **do not have any stable identifier**. They are keyed only by array index (`i` in `renderDeck`). The slide objects in `content.json` have no `id`, `uuid`, or `slug` fields—only `type`, content properties, and a `notes` object.

**Critical: Duplicate operation breaks index keying.** Line 918 in `main.js` shows the duplicate handler uses `splice(i + 1, 0, clone)`, which shifts all subsequent indices. The code then mirrors this for `slideShapes` overrides (lines 920–927), showing that the app assumes **per-slide metadata is keyed by index and must be shifted on insertion/deletion.**

**Conclusion:** Keying audio files by index will break on reorder/duplicate unless you either (a) add stable UUIDs to each slide, or (b) shift the audio file keys the same way `slideShapes` is shifted in the duplicate handler (lines 920–927).

---

### 2. **Static File Serving**
`vite.config.js` (lines 156–162) has:
- `root: '.'` (project root)
- No `publicDir` or `server.fs` restrictions specified

**Vite default:** serves the project root as static files. A directory `deck-generator/voiceovers/` would indeed be reachable as `/voiceovers/...` without configuration.

**No `.gitignore` exists** in the deck-generator directory (checked; file not found). You'll need to add one if you want to exclude voiceovers from version control.

---

### 3. **Play Mode UI Structure**
`enterPlayMode()` (lines 1032–1049) creates the overlay and renders it. `renderPlaySlide()` (lines 1061–1133) populates:
- `#play-label` (line 1109–1110): slide metadata at top-left
- `#play-slide` (line 1104–1106): the slide content (inside `.play-slide-container`)
- `#play-counter` (line 1112–1113): pagination info at bottom-right

**Keyboard:** Only Arrow Left/Right and Escape are bound (lines 1144–1152). **Spacebar is unbound** in play mode; no conflict there.

**CSS positions** (lines 1364–1382):
- `.play-label`: `position: fixed; top: 1.2rem; left: 1.5rem; pointer-events: none`
- `.play-counter`: `position: fixed; bottom: 1.2rem; right: 1.5rem; pointer-events: none`

**Suggested placement for per-slide audio controls:** Bottom-left corner (unused quadrant), or as an overlay button inside the slide container (above the counter). The current layout has top-left and bottom-right reserved.

---

### 4. **Slide Label Row Append Order**
Lines 936–940 in `main.js`:
1. `labelText` (slide number + type + layout name)
2. `shapeSelect` (dropdown for shape override)
3. `editBtn` (Edit/Close toggle)
4. `dupeBtn` (Duplicate)
5. `exportBtn` (Export PNG)

The `shapeSelect` is positioned with `margin-left: auto` (line 339 in style.css), pushing buttons to the right. A new voiceover button should slot **between `dupeBtn` and `exportBtn`** or **before `shapeSelect`** depending on intent.

---

### 5. **CSS Classes for Label Row & Play Overlay**

| Class | File:Line | Description |
|-------|-----------|-------------|
| `.slide-label` | `src/style.css:254` | `display: flex; justify-content: space-between` container for slide controls |
| `.slide-export-btn` | `src/style.css:355` | Generic button style (border, transparent bg, hover highlight) |
| `.slide-export-btn:hover` | `src/style.css:368` | Color/border highlight on hover |
| `.slide-export-btn:disabled` | `src/style.css:373` | Opacity 0.5, cursor: default |
| `#play-overlay` | `src/style.css:1334` | `position: fixed; inset: 0; z-index: 9999; background: #000` (fullscreen) |
| `.play-label` | `src/style.css:1364` | `position: fixed; top/left` metadata text, `pointer-events: none` |
| `.play-counter` | `src/style.css:1374` | `position: fixed; bottom/right` pagination, `pointer-events: none` |

Add voiceover CSS after line 1382 (end of `.play-counter` rule).

---

### 6. **Server-Side Write Pattern**
Your read is **correct.** The `/api/save` endpoint (lines 139–151) accepts a single POST with both `content` and `config`, and overwrites them atomically via `backupAndWrite()`.

For audio + metadata:
- **Add a new `/api/save-voiceover` endpoint** that takes binary audio + filename, writes to `voiceovers/` directory
- **Do NOT reuse `backupAndWrite()`** for the audio endpoint—it's JSON-only (line 15)
- **Keep `/api/save` for content.json updates** (e.g., updating `slide.audioUrl`)
- Client flow: (1) record → (2) POST to `/api/save-voiceover` → (3) update `slide.audioUrl` in `currentContent` → (4) POST to `/api/save` with updated content.json

The current `backupAndWrite()` creates timestamped backups of JSON. For audio files, you'd likely skip backups (disk space) or use a different strategy (version in filename or separate archive).

---

### 7. **Existing Notes Editor Pattern & Other Details**

**Notes pattern (lines 548–675):**
- Notes are stored on each slide as `slide.notes = { transition: string, points: array }`
- Built via `ensureSlideNotes()` helper (line 549) which initializes missing structure
- Panel built in `buildEditPanel()` with header "Speaker Notes", help text, and textarea fields for `transition` and individual `points`

**UX pattern to mirror:** The notes section sits at the bottom of the edit panel (grid-column: 1/-1), with a header and optional help text. A voiceover recorder could follow the same pattern—header + recorder UI + help text.

**Directory conventions:**
- `export/` — PNG exports (line 47, organized by aspect ratio: `export/16x9/`, `export/4x3/`)
- `output/` — Exists but not actively used in the codebase (likely for build artifacts)
- Suggested: `voiceovers/` at root level, flat structure keyed by slide index: `voiceovers/slide-001.webm`, etc., or by stable slide ID if you add UUIDs

**No existing audio infrastructure.** MediaRecorder and audio playback are not currently in the codebase.

---

## Summary for Implementation

**Critical constraint:** Slides are keyed by index only. Adding audio requires either (a) shifting indices on duplicate (mirror the `slideShapes` pattern), or (b) add stable UUIDs to all slides in `content.json`.

**Endpoints to add:** `/api/save-voiceover` (binary audio write) + reuse `/api/save` for content.json updates.

**Play mode UI space:** Bottom-left or inside slide container; top-left and bottom-right are reserved for metadata.

**Edit mode UI:** Slot voiceover control in label row (between duplicate & export buttons) OR below the notes panel in the edit pane following the notes UX pattern.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-13 07:42:47Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**Slides currently have no stable identifier — they're keyed only by array index, and `slideShapes` overrides are manually shifted on duplicate. Audio files need persistent paths, so how should I key them?**

Options:
- **Add stable IDs to every slide (Recommended)** - One-time migration: write a short ID (e.g. `id: "abc123"`) onto each of the 44 slides in content.json on first load and save. Audio files become voiceovers/abc123.webm. Reorder/duplicate becomes safe forever and you'll likely want stable IDs for other features later.
- **Mirror the slideShapes index-shift pattern** - Keep keying by index. Audio files are voiceovers/slide-03.webm. The Duplicate handler shifts audio file paths the same way it shifts slideShapes today. Smaller change, but reordering slides outside of duplicate (manually editing content.json) silently corrupts mappings.

**Where should the per-slide audio recorder UI live in edit view?**

Options:
- **Inline in the slide label row (Recommended)** - Sits next to Edit / Duplicate / Export PNG. Always visible without expanding anything — record / stop / play / duration / delete in one compact strip. Matches the pattern of the existing per-slide buttons.
- **Inside the collapsible edit panel** - Lives at the bottom of the Edit panel under Speaker Notes — only visible when you click Edit. Quieter UI, but two clicks to record and you can't see at a glance which slides have voiceovers.
- **Both** - Compact button + duration in the label row, fuller controls (re-record, transcript, etc.) in the edit panel. More work but most discoverable.

**When you click Duplicate on a slide that has a voiceover, what should happen to the audio?**

Options:
- **Clone has no voiceover (Recommended)** - The cloned slide starts empty — you'll re-record. Cleanest because the duplicate is usually a starting point you'll edit, and audio rarely transfers verbatim.
- **Copy the audio file to the new slide** - Server copies the audio file to the new slide's ID/index. Useful if you duplicate to make near-identical variants and want the same voiceover by default.

**Should the controls bar at the top show a total deck runtime (sum of all slide voiceover durations)?**

Options:
- **Yes — show total + recorded count (Recommended)** - e.g. `Voiceover: 12:34 · 18/44 slides`. Cheap to compute from the cached durations and very useful for keynote rehearsal pacing.
- **No — keep it slide-local only** - Just show duration per slide. Less UI clutter at the top.


**Answer:** Add stable IDs to every slide (Recommended), Inline in the slide label row (Recommended), Clone has no voiceover (Recommended), Yes — show total + recorded count (Recommended)
</details></tool-use>

```md
# Per-slide voiceover recording for the deck generator

## Context

The deck-generator already supports per-slide editing, duplication, PNG/PPTX/PDF export, and a fullscreen Play mode. The user is preparing the Teesside keynote and wants to rehearse with audio attached to each slide: record a voiceover in the browser, see how long the take is for each slide, and see total deck runtime so they can pace the talk. The voiceovers are for **rehearsal**, not for embedding into the exported PPTX/PDF (that's an out-of-scope follow-up).

Key context confirmed by exploration:
- Slides currently have **no stable ID** — they're addressed by array index. `slideShapes` overrides are manually shifted on Duplicate (`main.js:920-927`). Audio file paths can't safely live on indices, so a one-time migration to add stable IDs is the cleanest fix and the user has approved it.
- Vite serves the project root by default, so files dropped into `deck-generator/voiceovers/` are reachable as `/voiceovers/...` with no extra config.
- The browser-native `MediaRecorder` API + `getUserMedia` covers recording with **zero new dependencies**.
- The existing `/api/save` endpoint already handles content.json writes via `backupAndWrite`. The new audio endpoint should only handle the **binary file**; the client updates `content.json` and POSTs `/api/save` afterwards using the existing pattern.

## Approach summary

1. One-time migration: assign a stable 8-char ID to every slide on first load and persist via the existing Save flow.
2. Browser `MediaRecorder` records audio per slide; client posts the binary to a new `/api/save-voiceover` Vite endpoint that writes to `deck-generator/voiceovers/<slideId>.<ext>`.
3. Slide label row gets a compact recorder strip: Record / Play / Duration / Delete.
4. Top controls bar gains a `Voiceover: m:ss · X/Y` summary chip.
5. Duplicate produces a clone with **no** voiceover and a fresh ID.
6. Same recorder strip mirrored into Play mode (bottom-left of the slide container — currently unused per exploration).

## Files to modify

| File | Why |
|---|---|
| `deck-generator/src/main.js` | ID migration, voiceover state machine, UI fragments, total-runtime readout, duplicate guard |
| `deck-generator/vite.config.js` | Two new middleware endpoints (`/api/save-voiceover`, `/api/delete-voiceover`) |
| `deck-generator/src/style.css` | Voiceover button row, pulsing record indicator, summary chip |
| `deck-generator/content.json` | Auto-migrated by the client on first load (each slide gains an `id`) |
| `deck-generator/.gitignore` | New file: ignore `node_modules/`, `voiceovers/`, `export/`, `output/` (audio bloats git) |

## Step-by-step

### 1. Slide ID migration

In `init()` at `src/main.js:133`, after parsing `content.json`:
- New helper `ensureSlideId(slide)` mirrors the existing `ensureSlideNotes` pattern (`main.js:548`).
- `generateId()` returns 8 random base36 chars: `Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 10)`. 44 slides × 8 chars = collision risk negligible; loop on collision just in case.
- Iterate `content.slides`, call `ensureSlideId(s)` on each.
- If any ID was newly added, call `markDirty()` so the user saves on the next interaction. New IDs flow through the existing `/api/save` endpoint — no server change needed for the migration itself.

### 2. Voiceover data model

New optional field on each slide:
\```json
"voiceover": {
  "file": "voiceovers/abc12345.webm",
  "duration": 42.7,
  "mime": "audio/webm;codecs=opus",
  "updated": "2026-04-13T14:32:11.000Z"
}
\```
- `duration` is cached so the UI renders instantly without decoding the file each load.
- `file` is a relative URL the browser can fetch directly.
- `mime` lets `<audio>` know what to expect on Safari fallback.

### 3. Recording state machine (module-level in `src/main.js`)

\```js
let recordingState = null; // { slideIndex, slideId, recorder, chunks, startedAt, timerHandle }
let mediaStream = null;    // shared; released after each stop
\```

- **`startRecording(slideIndex)`**:
  1. If another slide is recording, `await stopRecording()` first.
  2. Lazy-acquire `mediaStream` via `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true })`. On `NotAllowedError`, show an inline error and bail.
  3. Pick MIME via `MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported`: prefer `audio/webm;codecs=opus`, fall back to `audio/webm`, then `audio/mp4` (Safari). If none, disable buttons with tooltip.
  4. Create `MediaRecorder`, push to `chunks` on `dataavailable`, store `startedAt = performance.now()`.
  5. Start an rAF-driven timer that updates the duration span live (`m:ss`).
  6. Toggle button to Stop, add `.voiceover-recording` class to slide wrapper.

- **`stopRecording()`**:
  1. `recorder.stop()` and await its `stop` event.
  2. Build `Blob` from `chunks`. Decode with a transient `AudioContext().decodeAudioData(arrayBuffer)` to get exact duration in seconds (the rAF timer is approximate). Close the AudioContext.
  3. Convert Blob to base64 dataURL.
  4. POST `/api/save-voiceover` with `{ slideId, dataUrl, mime, ext }`.
  5. On success: write the new `voiceover` object onto `currentContent.slides[slideIndex]`, `markDirty()`, re-render the slide label, call `updateVoiceoverSummary()`.
  6. Release tracks: `mediaStream.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop()); mediaStream = null;`
  7. Clear `recordingState`.

- **`playVoiceover(slideIndex)`**: create a transient `<audio src={slide.voiceover.file}>`, call `.play()`, drop reference on `ended`.

- **`deleteVoiceover(slideIndex)`**: `window.confirm`, POST `/api/delete-voiceover` with `{ slideId, file }`, on success `delete slide.voiceover`, `markDirty()`, re-render label, refresh summary.

### 4. UI fragment: `buildVoiceoverControls(slide, slideIndex)`

Returns a `DocumentFragment` containing:
- `<span class="voiceover-row">`
  - Record/Stop button (`.voiceover-btn`, reuses `.slide-export-btn` styling)
  - Play button (`▶`, disabled when no take)
  - `<span class="voiceover-duration">` — shows `m:ss` or `—`
  - Delete button (`✕`, disabled when no take)

Wired into `renderDeck` `labelRow` (`main.js:936-940`):
\```js
labelRow.appendChild(labelText);
labelRow.appendChild(shapeSelect);
labelRow.appendChild(editBtn);
labelRow.appendChild(dupeBtn);
labelRow.appendChild(buildVoiceoverControls(slide, i));  // NEW
labelRow.appendChild(exportBtn);
\```

Mirrored into `renderPlaySlide` (`main.js:1108`): append into a new `.play-voiceover` element absolutely positioned bottom-left of `.play-slide-container` (the `top-left` and `bottom-right` slots are taken by `.play-label` and `.play-counter`; `pointer-events` need to be re-enabled on this one since they're disabled on siblings).

### 5. Top controls bar — total runtime readout

In `renderControls` (`src/main.js:178-188`), add to `.ctrl-header-actions`:
\```html
<span id="voiceover-summary" class="ctrl-summary">Voiceover: 0:00 · 0/44</span>
\```

New helper `updateVoiceoverSummary()`:
- Iterates `currentContent.slides`, sums `voiceover?.duration ?? 0`, counts slides with a voiceover.
- Updates `#voiceover-summary` text content.
- Called from: end of `init`, after every successful record, after every delete.

### 6. Duplicate guard (`src/main.js:916`)

Inside the existing duplicate handler:
\```js
const clone = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(slide));
clone.id = generateId();           // NEW — fresh stable ID
delete clone.voiceover;            // NEW — clone starts with no audio
currentContent.slides.splice(i + 1, 0, clone);
\```
The existing `slideShapes` index-shift loop stays as-is. Audio is keyed by ID, so it doesn't need shifting.

### 7. Server endpoints in `vite.config.js`

Add inside `savePlugin().configureServer(server)`:

**`POST /api/save-voiceover`** — body `{ slideId, dataUrl, mime, ext }`:
- Validate `slideId` against `/^[a-z0-9]{4,32}$/i` (path-traversal guard).
- Validate `ext` against `/^(webm|mp4|m4a|ogg)$/`.
- `const voiceoversDir = path.resolve('voiceovers')`. Create if missing.
- Decode base64: `Buffer.from(dataUrl.split(',')[1], 'base64')`.
- `fs.writeFileSync(path.join(voiceoversDir, ` `${slideId}.${ext}` ` ), buf)`.
- If a file with a different ext exists for this slideId (e.g. switched from webm to mp4), unlink the old one.
- Return `{ ok: true, file: 'voiceovers/<slideId>.<ext>' }`.
- **No `backupAndWrite`** — the previous take is intentionally being replaced. Audio backups would explode disk usage.

**`POST /api/delete-voiceover`** — body `{ slideId, file }`:
- Validate `slideId`.
- Resolve `file` and verify with `path.resolve(file).startsWith(path.resolve('voiceovers'))` to block traversal.
- `fs.unlinkSync` if exists. Return `{ ok: true }`.

Vite already serves the project root, so `<audio src="voiceovers/abc12345.webm">` works with no `publicDir` change.

- **`ensureSlideNotes`** (`src/main.js:548`) — exact pattern for the new `ensureSlideId`.

1. **Boot:** `cd deck-generator && npm run dev`. Browser opens; check console for errors.
2. **Migration:** Save button becomes enabled on first load (IDs added). Click Save. Inspect `content.json` — every slide has an `id`. Reload — Save stays disabled.
3. **Record:** Click Record on slide 1. Browser prompts for mic permission. Speak for ~5s. Click Stop. Duration label shows `0:05`. File appears at `deck-generator/voiceovers/<id>.webm`. Top bar updates to `Voiceover: 0:05 · 1/44`.
4. **Play:** Click ▶ on slide 1. Audio plays back through speakers.
5. **Re-record:** Click Record again, speak 3s, Stop. New file replaces old, label shows `0:03`.
6. **Delete:** Click ✕, confirm dialog. File removed from disk, label resets to `—`, top bar count decrements.

10. **Permission denied:** Revoke mic permission in browser settings, click Record. UI shows error gracefully, no crash, mediaStream not leaked.

## Risks / notes for the implementer

- **MediaRecorder MIME detection** — Safari pre-2024 doesn't support `audio/webm`. The codec fallback chain handles this; if none supported, disable Record with a tooltip rather than crashing.
- **AudioContext lifecycle** — create lazily inside `stopRecording`, decode, then `ctx.close()`. Avoids leaving contexts hanging.
- **Mic permission** — Vite dev server is on `localhost` which is a secure context; `getUserMedia` works without HTTPS.
- **Slide ID collision** — 36⁸ ≈ 2.8 trillion. For 44 slides, collision risk is negligible. `ensureSlideId` should still loop on the unlikely case (check against the set of existing IDs).
- **One recorder at a time** — `startRecording` aborts any in-flight recording. There's no UI to record two slides simultaneously, which is correct for a single-mic rehearsal flow.
- **PPTX/PDF audio embedding** is out of scope. Flag in handoff that PptxGenJS supports `addAudio` if the user wants this later.

## Approved Plan:
# Per-slide voiceover recording for the deck generator

## Context

The deck-generator already supports per-slide editing, duplication, PNG/PPTX/PDF export, and a fullscreen Play mode. The user is preparing the Teesside keynote and wants to rehearse with audio attached to each slide: record a voiceover in the browser, see how long the take is for each slide, and see total deck runtime so they can pace the talk. The voiceovers are for **rehearsal**, not for embedding into the exported PPTX/PDF (that's an out-of-scope follow-up).

Key context confirmed by exploration:
- Slides currently have **no stable ID** — they're addressed by array index. `slideShapes` overrides are manually shifted on Duplicate (`main.js:920-927`). Audio file paths can't safely live on indices, so a one-time migration to add stable IDs is the cleanest fix and the user has approved it.
- Vite serves the project root by default, so files dropped into `deck-generator/voiceovers/` are reachable as `/voiceovers/...` with no extra config.
- The browser-native `MediaRecorder` API + `getUserMedia` covers recording with **zero new dependencies**.
- The existing `/api/save` endpoint already handles content.json writes via `backupAndWrite`. The new audio endpoint should only handle the **binary file**; the client updates `content.json` and POSTs `/api/save` afterwards using the existing pattern.

## Approach summary

1. One-time migration: assign a stable 8-char ID to every slide on first load and persist via the existing Save flow.
2. Browser `MediaRecorder` records audio per slide; client posts the binary to a new `/api/save-voiceover` Vite endpoint that writes to `deck-generator/voiceovers/<slideId>.<ext>`.
3. Slide label row gets a compact recorder strip: Record / Play / Duration / Delete.
4. Top controls bar gains a `Voiceover: m:ss · X/Y` summary chip.
5. Duplicate produces a clone with **no** voiceover and a fresh ID.
6. Same recorder strip mirrored into Play mode (bottom-left of the slide container — currently unused per exploration).

## Files to modify

| File | Why |
|---|---|
| `deck-generator/src/main.js` | ID migration, voiceover state machine, UI fragments, total-runtime readout, duplicate guard |
| `deck-generator/vite.config.js` | Two new middleware endpoints (`/api/save-voiceover`, `/api/delete-voiceover`) |
| `deck-generator/src/style.css` | Voiceover button row, pulsing record indicator, summary chip |
| `deck-generator/content.json` | Auto-migrated by the client on first load (each slide gains an `id`) |
| `deck-generator/.gitignore` | New file: ignore `node_modules/`, `voiceovers/`, `export/`, `output/` (audio bloats git) |

## Step-by-step

### 1. Slide ID migration

In `init()` at `src/main.js:133`, after parsing `content.json`:
- New helper `ensureSlideId(slide)` mirrors the existing `ensureSlideNotes` pattern (`main.js:548`).
- `generateId()` returns 8 random base36 chars: `Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 10)`. 44 slides × 8 chars = collision risk negligible; loop on collision just in case.
- Iterate `content.slides`, call `ensureSlideId(s)` on each.
- If any ID was newly added, call `markDirty()` so the user saves on the next interaction. New IDs flow through the existing `/api/save` endpoint — no server change needed for the migration itself.

### 2. Voiceover data model

New optional field on each slide:
```json
"voiceover": {
  "file": "voiceovers/abc12345.webm",
  "duration": 42.7,
  "mime": "audio/webm;codecs=opus",
  "updated": "2026-04-13T14:32:11.000Z"
}
```
- `duration` is cached so the UI renders instantly without decoding the file each load.
- `file` is a relative URL the browser can fetch directly.
- `mime` lets `<audio>` know what to expect on Safari fallback.

### 3. Recording state machine (module-level in `src/main.js`)

```js
let recordingState = null; // { slideIndex, slideId, recorder, chunks, startedAt, timerHandle }
let mediaStream = null;    // shared; released after each stop
```

- **`startRecording(slideIndex)`**:
  1. If another slide is recording, `await stopRecording()` first.
  2. Lazy-acquire `mediaStream` via `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true })`. On `NotAllowedError`, show an inline error and bail.
  3. Pick MIME via `MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported`: prefer `audio/webm;codecs=opus`, fall back to `audio/webm`, then `audio/mp4` (Safari). If none, disable buttons with tooltip.
  4. Create `MediaRecorder`, push to `chunks` on `dataavailable`, store `startedAt = performance.now()`.
  5. Start an rAF-driven timer that updates the duration span live (`m:ss`).
  6. Toggle button to Stop, add `.voiceover-recording` class to slide wrapper.

- **`stopRecording()`**:
  1. `recorder.stop()` and await its `stop` event.
  2. Build `Blob` from `chunks`. Decode with a transient `AudioContext().decodeAudioData(arrayBuffer)` to get exact duration in seconds (the rAF timer is approximate). Close the AudioContext.
  3. Convert Blob to base64 dataURL.
  4. POST `/api/save-voiceover` with `{ slideId, dataUrl, mime, ext }`.
  5. On success: write the new `voiceover` object onto `currentContent.slides[slideIndex]`, `markDirty()`, re-render the slide label, call `updateVoiceoverSummary()`.
  6. Release tracks: `mediaStream.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop()); mediaStream = null;`
  7. Clear `recordingState`.

- **`playVoiceover(slideIndex)`**: create a transient `<audio src={slide.voiceover.file}>`, call `.play()`, drop reference on `ended`.

- **`deleteVoiceover(slideIndex)`**: `window.confirm`, POST `/api/delete-voiceover` with `{ slideId, file }`, on success `delete slide.voiceover`, `markDirty()`, re-render label, refresh summary.

### 4. UI fragment: `buildVoiceoverControls(slide, slideIndex)`

Returns a `DocumentFragment` containing:
- `<span class="voiceover-row">`
  - Record/Stop button (`.voiceover-btn`, reuses `.slide-export-btn` styling)
  - Play button (`▶`, disabled when no take)
  - `<span class="voiceover-duration">` — shows `m:ss` or `—`
  - Delete button (`✕`, disabled when no take)

Wired into `renderDeck` `labelRow` (`main.js:936-940`):

labelRow.appendChild(shapeSelect);
labelRow.appendChild(editBtn);
labelRow.appendChild(dupeBtn);
labelRow.appendChild(buildVoiceoverControls(slide, i));  // NEW

- Iterates `currentContent.slides`, sums `voiceover?.duration ?? 0`, counts slides with a voiceover.
- Updates `#voiceover-summary` text content.
- Called from: end of `init`, after every successful record, after every delete.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-13 07:55:56Z)**_

Implementation complete. Summary of what changed:

**Files**
- `vite.config.js` — added `/api/save-voiceover` and `/api/delete-voiceover` middleware with `slideId` regex + path-traversal guards.
- `src/main.js` — slide ID migration (`ensureSlideId`/`generateSlideId`/`formatDuration`), `MediaRecorder`-based recording state machine (`startRecording`/`stopRecording`/`playVoiceover`/`deleteVoiceover`), `buildVoiceoverControls` UI factory, `updateVoiceoverSummary` for the top chip, label-row + Play-mode wiring, and a Duplicate-handler guard that gives clones a fresh ID and no audio.
- `src/style.css` — `.voiceover-row`, `.voiceover-btn` (with pulsing red `is-recording` state), `.voiceover-duration`, `.ctrl-summary` chip, and Play-mode-specific overrides for the bottom-left controls.
- `.gitignore` — new file ignoring `node_modules/` and `voiceovers/`.

**Smoke tested:** `node --check` on both edited JS files, Vite boots cleanly, `/`, `/src/main.js`, `/src/style.css` all 200, save → static GET → delete round-trip works, and all three security guards (bad slideId, bad ext, traversal path) reject. Mic-dependent flows (record/playback) need a real browser to verify — open `http://localhost:5173` (or whatever Vite picks), the Save button should light up immediately because the slide ID migration runs on first load.

**Note:** `voiceovers/` is gitignored — flip that line if you want takes committed for backup.

page.on('pageerror', err => errors.push(`PAGE ERROR: ${err.message}`));
await page.goto('http://localhost:5173/', { waitUntil: 'networkidle' });
await page.waitForTimeout(800);

// Check the top-bar Play button

console.log(`btn-play exists: ${topPlay}`);

console.log(`.voiceover-row exists: ${firstVoRow}`);

const playDisabled = await firstPlayBtn.isDisabled().catch(() => 'n/a');

await page.click('#btn-play');
await page.waitForTimeout(500);
const playOverlayExists = await page.locator('#play-overlay').count();
const playSlideHTML = await page.locator('#play-slide').innerHTML().catch(() => 'NOT FOUND');
console.log(`#play-overlay exists: ${playOverlayExists}`);
console.log(`#play-slide content length: ${playSlideHTML.length}`);

const playVoChildren = await page.locator('#play-voiceover .voiceover-row').count();
console.log(`#play-voiceover exists: ${playVoSlot}, has voiceover-row child: ${playVoChildren}`);

console.log('--- console logs ---');
console.log(logs.join('\n') || '(none)');
console.log('--- page errors ---');
console.log(errors.join('\n') || '(none)');

await browser.close();
EOF

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-13 08:01:23Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-13 08:01:27Z)**_

Found it! The user clarified: it's the per-slide ▶ play button. The bug is in my server-side base64 strip regex.

When MediaRecorder uses `audio/webm;codecs=opus`, the resulting dataURL is `data:audio/webm;codecs=opus;base64,<data>`. My regex `/^data:[^;]+;base64,/` stops at the first `;` (after `audio/webm`) and then expects `base64,` immediately — but it gets `codecs=opus;base64,`. The match fails, the replace is a no-op, and we decode the entire dataURL prefix-and-all as base64, writing a corrupt file. Plays silently.

Fix: use `/^data:[^,]+,/` which strips up to the first `,`.

I'll also add an error log to `playVoiceover` for future visibility, and match the voiceover button dimensions to `.slide-export-btn`.

Fix verified — file is exactly 123 bytes (the decoded payload), not the corrupt larger size you'd get from decoding the prefix-and-all. Server restart picked it up cleanly.

**Important note for the user:** any voiceovers you recorded *before* this fix are corrupt on disk — you'll need to **delete and re-record them** for playback to work. Hit the ✕ on those slides and record again. New takes will play correctly.

Both fixes:
- **Play voiceover bug** — `vite.config.js`: replaced the brittle `^data:[^;]+;base64,` regex with a comma-based split that correctly handles parameterised MIMEs like `audio/webm;codecs=opus`. Plus added an `audio.error` log in `playVoiceover` so silent failures surface in the console next time.
- **Button sizes** — `style.css`: `.voiceover-btn` now uses `padding: 0.25rem 0.6rem` and `color: #666` to match `.slide-export-btn` exactly.

Server is running at http://localhost:5173/ — refresh the page to pick up the JS/CSS changes.

cd "/Users/stugrey/Documents/Student Voice ICD/Conferences:Webinars/Teesside Keynote" && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Per-slide voiceover recording, plus LFS for audio

- Browser MediaRecorder feature: per-slide record / play / duration / delete
  controls in the slide label row and play-mode overlay, with a top-bar
  total-runtime + recorded-count chip
- New /api/save-voiceover and /api/delete-voiceover Vite endpoints with
  slideId regex and path-traversal guards; data-URL stripping handles
  parameterised MIME types (audio/webm;codecs=opus)
- Stable 8-char slide ids migrated into content.json so audio file paths
  survive reorder and duplicate; Duplicate now mints a fresh id and clears
  the clone's voiceover
- voiceovers/*.webm|ogg|mp4|m4a tracked via git LFS (.gitattributes at repo
  root); deck-generator/.gitignore added for node_modules
- Bundled: pending content edits, anki build artefacts, and practice PDFs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1

<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```javascript
#!/usr/bin/env node
// Transcribe per-slide voiceovers with OpenAI Whisper and write the results
// back into content.json as `slide.voiceover.transcript`.
//
// Usage:
//   node scripts/transcribe-voiceovers.js              # transcribe all untranscribed
//   node scripts/transcribe-voiceovers.js --force      # retranscribe everything
//   node scripts/transcribe-voiceovers.js --limit 1    # stop after N slides
//   node scripts/transcribe-voiceovers.js --dry-run    # show what would run
//
// Reads OPENAI_API_KEY from an .env file (default path below).
// Incremental save: writes content.json after every slide so a failure
// mid-run can be resumed just by re-running.

import fs from 'node:fs';
import path from 'node:path';
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';

const ENV_PATH = process.env.OPENAI_ENV_PATH
  || '/Users/stugrey/Documents/Student Voice ICD/configs/student-voice-eleventy/.env';

const __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);
const __dirname = path.dirname(__filename);
const ROOT = path.resolve(__dirname, '..');
const CONTENT_PATH = path.join(ROOT, 'content.json');

function loadEnv(envPath) {
  if (!fs.existsSync(envPath)) {
    throw new Error(`env file not found: ${envPath}`);
  }
  const src = fs.readFileSync(envPath, 'utf8');
  for (const raw of src.split('\n')) {
    const line = raw.trim();
    if (!line || line.startsWith('#')) continue;
    const m = line.match(/^(?:export\s+)?([A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*)\s*=\s*(.*)$/i);
    if (!m) continue;
    let [, key, val] = m;
    val = val.trim();
    if ((val.startsWith('"') && val.endsWith('"')) ||
        (val.startsWith("'") && val.endsWith("'"))) {
      val = val.slice(1, -1);
    }
    if (!(key in process.env)) process.env[key] = val;
  }
}

function parseArgs(argv) {
  const args = { force: false, dryRun: false, limit: Infinity };
  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i++) {
    const a = argv[i];
    if (a === '--force') args.force = true;
    else if (a === '--dry-run') args.dryRun = true;
    else if (a === '--limit') args.limit = Number(argv[++i]) || Infinity;
  }
  return args;
}

async function transcribeFile(filePath, mime) {
  const buf = fs.readFileSync(filePath);
  const fd = new FormData();
  fd.append('file', new Blob([buf], { type: mime || 'audio/webm' }), path.basename(filePath));
  fd.append('model', 'whisper-1');
  fd.append('response_format', 'json');

  const res = await fetch('https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/transcriptions', {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { 'Authorization': `Bearer ${process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY}` },
    body: fd,
  });

  if (!res.ok) {
    const text = await res.text();
    throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}: ${text}`);
  }
  return res.json();
}

function writeContent(content) {
  fs.writeFileSync(CONTENT_PATH, JSON.stringify(content, null, 2) + '\n');
}

async function main() {
  const args = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2));

  loadEnv(ENV_PATH);
  if (!process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY) {
    console.error(`OPENAI_API_KEY not set (checked ${ENV_PATH})`);
    process.exit(1);
  }

  const content = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(CONTENT_PATH, 'utf8'));
  const slides = content.slides || [];

  let transcribed = 0;
  let skipped = 0;
  let failed = 0;

  for (let i = 0; i < slides.length; i++) {
    if (transcribed >= args.limit) break;
    const slide = slides[i];
    const vo = slide?.voiceover;
    const page = String(i + 1).padStart(2, '0');

    if (!vo?.file) {
      console.log(`slide ${page}: no voiceover — skip`);
      skipped++;
      continue;
    }
    if (vo.transcript && !args.force) {
      console.log(`slide ${page}: already transcribed — skip (--force to redo)`);
      skipped++;
      continue;
    }

    const filePath = path.join(ROOT, vo.file);
    if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
      console.warn(`slide ${page}: audio missing on disk — ${vo.file}`);
      failed++;
      continue;
    }

    if (args.dryRun) {
      console.log(`slide ${page}: would transcribe ${vo.file}`);
      continue;
    }

    process.stdout.write(`slide ${page}: transcribing ${path.basename(filePath)}... `);
    try {
      const result = await transcribeFile(filePath, vo.mime);
      vo.transcript = (result.text || '').trim();
      vo.transcribed_at = new Date().toISOString();
      transcribed++;
      // Save after each slide so we never lose more than one on failure
      writeContent(content);
      console.log(`${vo.transcript.length} chars ✓`);
    } catch (e) {
      failed++;
      console.log(`FAILED`);
      console.error(`  ${e.message}`);
    }
  }

  console.log();
  console.log(`done — transcribed: ${transcribed}, skipped: ${skipped}, failed: ${failed}`);
  if (failed > 0) process.exit(2);
}

main().catch(e => {
  console.error(e);
  process.exit(1);
});

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-13 18:57:22Z)**_

All 44 slides now carry both the clean `transcript` string and a `words` array with per-token `{ word, start, end }` in seconds. 6,077 word entries total; `content.json` grew to ~720 KB which is fine for a dev tool.

**Shape on each slide:**
```json
"voiceover": {
  "file": "voiceovers/xyz.webm",
  "duration": 34.14,
  "mime": "audio/webm;codecs=opus",
  "updated": "...",
  "transcript": "Hi, and good afternoon everyone...",
  "transcribed_at": "2026-04-13T...",
  "words": [
    { "word": "Hi",   "start": 0.0,  "end": 0.86 },
    { "word": "and",  "start": 1.04, "end": 1.20 },
    ...
  ]
}
```

**Caveats for when you build the highlighter:**
- Whisper attaches punctuation to the preceding word (e.g. `"Hi,"` not separate tokens) — so `words.map(w => w.word).join(' ')` gives you a version with double spaces around commas. Use `slide.voiceover.transcript` for display and the `words` array purely for timing/matching.
- When a slide is re-recorded via the UI, the client sets `slide.voiceover = {...}` and wipes the transcript + words. That's correct — the old timings belong to the old audio. You'd re-run `node scripts/transcribe-voiceovers.js` after re-recording.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-13 18:59:03Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-13 18:59:07Z)**_

Yes. Cleanest design: after a save succeeds, the client fires a second request to a new `/api/transcribe-voiceover` endpoint; when that returns, `slide.voiceover.transcript` and `.words` get merged in and `markDirty()` is called again so the next UI Save persists them. The audio save itself is not blocked on Whisper — if the API key is missing or Whisper is slow/down, playback still works; you just don't get a transcript.

Server-side, I'll load `OPENAI_API_KEY` from the same `.env` once when Vite boots.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-13 19:00:58Z)**_

Endpoint works end-to-end: env loaded, Whisper authenticated, `verbose_json` + word timestamps flowing back through the middleware. 

**How the auto-flow now works:**

1. User clicks Record → Stop on a slide
2. Client POSTs audio to `/api/save-voiceover` (existing flow) → file saved
3. Client immediately fires `/api/transcribe-voiceover` **without blocking**, and sets `.is-transcribing` on the row — the duration label shows e.g. `0:42 ⋯` pulsing cyan
4. Server reads the fresh `.webm`, calls Whisper, returns `{ transcript, words }`
5. Client merges them into `slide.voiceover`, calls `markDirty()` again — the Save button lights up
6. The `⋯` disappears; everything persists on the next UI Save

**Race safety:** the merge checks `slide.voiceover.file === <the file we sent>` before writing, so if you re-record the same slide mid-transcription the stale result is dropped.

**Failure modes handled:**
- No `OPENAI_API_KEY` → endpoint returns 400, client logs `warn`, voiceover still playable
- Whisper error → same, no alert (just a console.warn)
- Re-record during transcription → old result discarded

**Quick verify for you:** reload the browser page once to pick up the new client JS, then re-record any slide. You should see the label pulse `m:ss ⋯` for ~2-5s and then settle. Run this afterwards to confirm the transcript lands in `content.json` (no need to manually run the script anymore):

```
node -e 'const c = require("./content.json"); const i = 0; console.log(c.slides[i].voiceover.transcript)'
```

  background transcription call; merge transcript + word timings back
  onto the slide and re-mark dirty so the next UI Save persists them.

  a transcription is in flight
